description_long: This object is a pair of scissors with a simple, utilitarian design, featuring two elongated, straight blades joined at a central pivot. The handles are oval-shaped loops, both of equal size, allowing for ambidextrous use and comfortable grip for most hand sizes. The blades are slender and taper to a sharp, pointed tip, suitable for precision cutting tasks. The entire object appears to be made from a uniform metallic material, giving it a smooth, matte gray finish. There are no visible coatings, padding, or ergonomic enhancements on the handles, and the pivot joint is a basic, flush rivet. No brand markings or decorative elements are present, emphasizing a functional and minimalist aesthetic.
description: A minimalist pair of metal scissors with equal-sized oval handles and pointed blades, joined by a simple rivet. The surface is smooth and matte gray, with no visible branding or ergonomic features.
description_view_0: Side view showing both oval handles and the full length of the straight, pointed blades.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the sharp blade tips and the open configuration of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the symmetry of the handles and the central pivot joint.
description_view_3: Angled view from the handle side, emphasizing the finger loops and the alignment of the blades.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical oval handles, uncoated, ambidextrous, no ergonomic or special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Flush rivet joint, non-adjustable, visible at the intersection of the blades.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Equal-sized oval loops, smooth surface, no inserts or anatomical contours, accommodates one finger per loop.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed tip, designed for precision cutting, not rounded or blunted.
